title: fix: switch gemini-3.1-flash-lite to GA model id (preview retired)
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Google retired the `gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview` Vertex publisher model → `gemini-flash-lite-3.1` and `gemini-search-fast` returned 502 (upstream 404) in production
- Switch portkey config + registry `modelId` to GA id `gemini-3.1-flash-lite` (verified working via direct Vertex probe with the prod SA, `global` location)
- Old `-preview` alias kept in registry for existing callers
- Probed all other Vertex Gemini models: only flash-lite preview was retired

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

gist.headline: Gemini Flash Lite graduates
gist.blurb: Google retired the preview version of Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite, so we've upgraded our endpoints to the shiny new General Availability model. Fast, lightweight generations are back in action!
gist.summary: Updated the Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite and Gemini Search Fast endpoints to use the General Availability model ID (gemini-3.1-flash-lite) after Google retired the preview version.
gist.impact: Calls to gemini-flash-lite-3.1 and gemini-search-fast will no longer return 502 errors, and existing callers using the -preview alias are automatically routed to the GA model.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot swapping out a flickering gem (preview) for a bright, stable glowing gem (GA) inside a cozy retro server rack.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot carefully swapping a flickering blue gem for a bright, glowing stable gem inside a retro server rack. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
